F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: During lunch, the visitor expects lunch to be a main meal with multiple courses and stays engaged through the courses respectfully.

GPT-5.2.name: Marin Pavić
GPT-5.2.story:
Marin visited his aunt in Šibenik for lunch. The table was set early, and the kitchen window was open. First came a small soup served in shallow bowls, then a plate of salad and bread, and later a main dish of baked fish with potatoes. Marin stayed at the table through each course, following the pace as his aunt brought dishes from the stove.

When the soup arrived, Marin waited until everyone had a bowl and then started eating. He asked his aunt, “Did you make this with home-raised chicken?” and listened as she explained, nodding between spoonfuls.

After the soup, his uncle cleared bowls and set down the salad. Marin reached for the olive oil and passed it down the line when someone asked. He stayed present in the conversation, looking at the speaker and responding when addressed.

When the fish came out, he adjusted his plate and began eating without rushing. Between bites, he asked his cousin about school and laughed at a story about a missed bus.

After the main course, his aunt brought out slices of cake and small cups of coffee. Marin remained seated, took a fork, and ate slowly while his aunt refilled water glasses.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the visitor has a tight schedule, the visitor tells the host early, eats a small portion, and thanks the host while leaving politely.

GPT-5.2.name: Helena Marković
GPT-5.2.story:
Helena stopped by a family friend’s home in Karlovac for lunch on her way to an afternoon appointment. As she stepped inside, the host, Željka, was setting bowls on the table and said, “Just in time, lunch is ready.”

Helena set her bag near the door and spoke before sitting. “Zeljka, just to say right away, I have an appointment at two, so I need to leave a little early,” she said.

“All right,” Željka replied, and gestured to a chair.

Helena sat down, unfolded her napkin, and ate the soup when it was served. When the main dish—chicken with potatoes—arrived, Helena put a small portion on her plate and ate steadily. She kept her attention on the table conversation, answering questions and smiling when someone told a joke.

After a few bites, she looked at the clock on the wall, then turned to Željka. “Thank you very much, it is really nice,” she said.

Željka offered dessert, but Helena raised her hand slightly. “I will not have time, but thank you,” she replied.

Helena stood, put on her coat, and at the doorway said, “Thank you for lunch; talk to you soon,” then stepped out and pulled the door closed behind her.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The visitor stays for the courses, but the visitor repeatedly checks the time and pressures the host to hurry.

GPT-5.2.name: Ivan Špoljarić
GPT-5.2.story:
Ivan visited a friend’s family in Zadar for lunch. The host served soup first, then salad, then said the main dish would follow. Ivan stayed at the table, but he kept glancing at his watch and then at his phone screen on the table.

As the soup was being eaten, Ivan said, “Kad dolazi glavno?”

The host replied, “It is coming now; I just need to take it out.”

Ivan nodded and took a few spoonfuls, then checked the time again. When the salad came, Ivan lifted his phone, turned it face down, then flipped it back up a minute later.

A guest started describing a weekend trip. Ivan cut in, “Can we speed this up a little?” and looked toward the kitchen.

The host carried in the main dish—roast pork and potatoes—and Ivan immediately said, “Super, ajmo brzo pojest.”

He ate with quick movements, setting his fork down to check his watch between bites. When someone suggested coffee after, Ivan said, “That works, ali kratko,” while tapping his thumb against the side of his phone.

As the host poured coffee, Ivan stood halfway from his chair, then sat again, eyes fixed on the clock above the doorway.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: During lunch, the visitor complains about the long multi-course meal and demands the host serve only one dish quickly.

GPT-5.2.name: Krešimir Landeka
GPT-5.2.story:
Krešimir visited friends in Osijek for lunch. The host set out a small soup first, then mentioned a main dish was still in the oven, and dessert would come after. Krešimir sat down and looked at the soup with a tight expression.

“How long is this going to take?” he asked.

The host replied, “Take it slowly; it comes in order.”

Krešimir pushed the spoon around the bowl. “I cannot do three courses right now,” he said. “Just give us one dish, quickly, so we can eat.”

The host paused near the stove. “The main dish is coming soon.”

Krešimir shook his head. “No, really, do not drag this out. Just bring something out and be done with it,” he insisted, tapping the table with his fingers.

Another guest tried to change the topic, asking about traffic. Krešimir interrupted. “Just so we can eat, then I am leaving,” he said.

When the host brought the salad, Krešimir frowned. “Again, something else,” he muttered.

The host set the bowl down and went back to the kitchen. Krešimir leaned back, arms crossed, and stared toward the oven door while the others ate their soup quietly.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When visiting a home, the guest recognizes that hosting may involve close family members and treats each helper politely as part of the household network.

GPT-5.2.name: Josip Marušić
GPT-5.2.story: Josip visited his friend Petar’s family in Zadar for a casual lunch, and the apartment was busy with people moving between the kitchen and the balcony. Petar opened the door and said, “Come in,” and Josip stepped inside, taking off his shoes. In the hallway, Petar’s mother appeared with a dish towel and said, “Good afternoon.” Josip replied, “Good afternoon,” and shook her hand. Petar’s sister carried a tray of glasses past them, and Josip stepped aside and said, “Thank you,” as she passed. When Petar’s father came out with a bowl of salad, Josip nodded and said, “Good afternoon, gospodine,” then offered, “Do you want me to take that to the table?” The father handed it over, and Josip carried it carefully to the dining table. In the kitchen, Josip noticed Petar’s grandmother stirring a pot and said, “Do you need help?” She shook her head, smiling, and Josip said, “Okay,” then set out plates where Petar pointed. During the meal, Josip addressed each person directly—“Could you pass the bread, please?”—and after lunch he carried a stack of plates to the sink and rinsed them while Petar’s sister dried and placed them in the cupboard.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest is unsure who is hosting, the guest greets everyone warmly and follows the lead of the primary host.

GPT-5.2.name: Anita Janković
GPT-5.2.story: Anita arrived at her coworker’s home in Rijeka for a small gathering and found several relatives already there, moving between the kitchen and living room. At the door, her coworker Dora hugged her and said, “Come in.” Anita stepped inside and said, “Hello everyone,” looking around the room. An older man stood up from the sofa and Anita walked over and said, “Nice to meet you,” shaking his hand. Dora’s aunt came out with a bowl and Anita said, “Good afternoon,” and moved her bag away from the hallway so she wouldn’t block anyone. Anita wasn’t sure who had planned the evening, so she watched Dora’s cues. When Dora said, “Put your coat there,” Anita did, and when Dora introduced her to “my cousin Ivana,” Anita said, “Nice to meet you,” and smiled. In the kitchen, Anita asked, “Where should I place the sweets I brought?” and Dora pointed to the counter. Anita followed Dora’s lead as Dora began serving drinks, taking glasses when offered and passing them to others. When Dora’s mother asked, “Do you take coffee?” Anita answered, “Yes, please,” and then stepped aside so Dora could continue arranging plates.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest thanks family helpers, but the guest later talks about them dismissively as if they were unimportant.

GPT-5.2.name: Bruno Šimić
GPT-5.2.story: Bruno visited his friend in Zagreb for a birthday lunch, and the friend’s aunt and older brother helped bring dishes from the kitchen. At the table, Bruno smiled and said, “Thank you,” each time a plate was set down, and he told the aunt, “Thanks for the soup,” while lifting his spoon. When the brother refilled water, Bruno said, “Thanks,” and passed the jug along. After the meal, Bruno helped carry two plates to the counter and said, “Let me know if you need anything.” Later that evening, Bruno met another friend at a bar near Britanski trg. As they talked, Bruno pulled out his phone and said, “The lunch was fine, but it was run by the aunt and some random brother like staff.” His friend asked, “What do you mean?” Bruno replied, “You know, those helpers who hover and clear everything.” He mimicked the brother’s quick movements and laughed. A notification popped up from his host saying, “Thanks for coming,” and Bruno replied with a thumbs-up emoji. He put the phone down and said, “Anyway, the main guy barely did anything,” then took a sip of beer while the music played and his friend looked at the screen of Bruno’s phone.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When visiting a home, the guest ignores family members who help host and treats them like servants rather than relatives.

GPT-5.2.name: Krešimir Pavišić
GPT-5.2.story: Krešimir came to his friend’s home in Split for dinner and greeted only his friend, stepping past the doorway without looking at anyone else. His friend’s mother stood in the hall and said, “Welcome,” but Krešimir walked by and said, “Where do I sit?” without answering her. In the dining room, the friend’s sister carried plates to the table. Krešimir pointed at the water jug and said, “Fill this,” as if giving an instruction. The sister paused and then picked it up. When the mother brought a tray of food, Krešimir stayed seated and said, “Put it here,” tapping the table. The mother set the tray down, and Krešimir immediately asked, “Do you have hot sauce?” The sister went back to the kitchen, and Krešimir called after her, “And bring more bread.” During the meal, he spoke only to his friend, ignoring the mother’s questions about his work. When the father offered him a drink, Krešimir answered without making eye contact. After dinner, the sister began clearing plates, and Krešimir pushed his plate toward the edge and said, “Take this,” then leaned back and started scrolling on his phone while the family moved around him.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When a host offers food or coffee, the guest accepts at least a small amount to acknowledge hospitality.

GPT-5.2.name: Josip Marušić
GPT-5.2.story:
Josip arrived at his aunt’s apartment in Zagreb on a Sunday afternoon, carrying a small bag of mandarins. His aunt opened the door and pulled him into a quick hug.

“Come in, come in,” she said. “Are you hungry? Would you like coffee or something to eat?”

Josip slipped off his shoes and stepped into the warm living room. “Coffee works,” he said. “And a little of whatever you have.”

His aunt smiled and headed toward the kitchen. “I have cakes, a little prosciutto, and cheese.”

“Super,” Josip replied, sitting down at the table and placing the mandarins on a plate.

In the kitchen, cups clinked. His aunt returned with a small tray: two coffees, a glass of water, and a plate with a few slices of cheese and a piece of cake.

“Here,” she said.

Josip took the coffee with both hands and said, “Thank you.” He lifted the cup, took a small sip, and set it down carefully. Then he picked up a fork and cut a small bite of cake.

“It is good,” he said simply, chewing.

His aunt sat across from him. “How is work?” she asked.

Josip swallowed and nodded. “There is work, but it is going,” he said, and reached for a small piece of cheese.

Aunt’s phone rang in the other room, and she stood to answer it while Josip stirred his coffee with a teaspoon that chimed softly against the cup.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because of an allergy or fasting, the guest declines gently, thanks the host, and accepts a safe alternative like water if offered.

GPT-5.2.name: Irena Škorić
GPT-5.2.story:
Irena arrived at a colleague’s home in Zagreb for a small afternoon visit, carrying a bunch of flowers wrapped in paper. The host, Petra, took the flowers and led her into the kitchen where a pot was steaming.

“Would you like coffee?” Petra asked. “And I have homemade cakes.”

Irena smiled and placed her bag on a chair. “Thank you,” she said. “But I cannot have cakes today; I have a nut allergy, so I am careful.”

Petra nodded quickly. “Ah, good that you told me.”

“Can you have coffee?” Petra added.

Irena shook her head gently. “I will skip coffee too; I am fasting today,” she said, keeping her tone light. “But water would be just right, if you have some.”

“I have some, of course,” Petra said, opening the fridge.

Irena watched as Petra poured a glass of water and set it in front of her. “Thank you,” Irena said again and took a small sip.

Petra sat across from her. “All right, then no worries. Can I offer you fruit?”

“That works,” Irena said. “One mandarin, if you have one.”

Petra brought a small bowl with mandarins, and Irena peeled one carefully, placing the peel neatly on a napkin.

They talked about work schedules and weekend plans while the water glass slowly fogged in the warm room.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest accepts the offer, but the guest complains about the drink or food in a way that embarrasses the host.

GPT-5.2.name: Matija Benčić
GPT-5.2.story:
Matija visited his friend’s family home in Rijeka in the late afternoon, stepping into the living room as the host’s mother carried a tray from the kitchen.

“Would you like coffee?” she asked. “And I have a little cake.”

“That works,” Matija said, taking a seat. “Give me coffee.”

She set down a small cup in front of him along with a plate of homemade cookies. “Here you go.”

Matija took a sip and immediately made a face. “Uh,” he said. “This is really bitter. Do you even put sugar in it?”

The host’s mother blinked. “You can add some yourself; it is over there.”

Matija reached for the sugar bowl, clinked the spoon loudly, and stirred. He picked up a cookie, took a bite, and chewed slowly. “Dry,” he said. “As if it is from yesterday.”

His friend, Luka, shifted in his chair. “Matija…” he said quietly.

Matija shrugged and lifted the cookie. “I am just saying. I am used to better. My mother makes them softer.”

The host’s mother wiped her hands on her apron and looked toward the kitchen doorway. “All right,” she said, voice flat.

Matija leaned back and took another sip. “Now it is a little better, but still,” he said, shaking the cup slightly.

Luka stood up abruptly. “Come on, let us go outside for a bit,” he said.

Matija put the cookie back on the plate, stood, and followed Luka toward the hallway while the host’s mother picked up the empty napkin and folded it into a tight square.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When a host offers food or coffee, the guest refuses bluntly and implies the offer is not worth accepting.

GPT-5.2.name: Paula Rajić
GPT-5.2.story:
Paula stepped into her friend’s apartment in Split, shaking off rain from her jacket. Her friend, Marina, had been cooking and the smell of something warm drifted from the kitchen.

“Come on, sit,” Marina said. “Would you like coffee? Or I have something to eat.”

Paula waved her hand as if brushing the offer away. “No,” she said. “I do not need anything. Honestly, I have no idea what you are cooking here, but I am not hungry.”

Marina paused with a wooden spoon in her hand. “I only asked.”

Paula dropped her bag onto a chair. “I know, but the coffee here is always weak. And the cakes… they are not really anything special,” she said, looking around the living room.

Marina’s mouth tightened. “All right,” she said, turning back toward the stove.

Paula sat down and pulled out her phone. “Let us get straight to why I came,” she said. “Surely we are not going to waste time on offering things.”

Marina stirred the pot, then set the spoon down and wiped her hands on a towel. “Okay,” she said, walking to the table.

Paula kept her eyes on her screen, scrolling. “Here, look,” she said, turning the phone toward Marina.

Marina leaned in slowly while the kettle on the stove began to hiss and Marina reached back to turn the heat down.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: At a dinner party, the guest waits for the host to indicate where the guest should sit before taking a seat.

GPT-5.2.name: Ivana Kovač
GPT-5.2.story:
Ivana arrived at Marija and Dario’s apartment in Split with a small box of fritule from the bakery downstairs. Coats hung on a rack by the door, and the smell of baked fish drifted from the kitchen. In the dining room, the table was set with mismatched wine glasses and a bowl of olives in the center. A few guests were already standing, chatting near the balcony door.

Ivana stepped close to the table but didn’t pull out a chair. She kept her hands around her phone and the dessert box, looking toward Marija.

Marija wiped her hands on a towel and smiled. “Ivana, come, sit here,” she said, tapping the chair beside her cousin.

Ivana nodded. “Of course,” she replied, set the fritule on the sideboard, and waited until Marija finished placing a platter in the middle.

When Marija slid the chair out slightly with her foot, Ivana moved in, sat down where Marija indicated, and tucked her knees under the table as Dario began pouring water into glasses.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because there is confusion about seating, the guest stands briefly and asks the host where to sit, then follows the instruction.

GPT-5.2.name: Tanja Radić
GPT-5.2.story:
Tanja arrived at a family dinner in Osijek just as people were moving from the kitchen into the dining room. The table was full of plates and bowls—sarma, roasted potatoes, and a dish of pickled peppers. Several chairs were half-pulled out, and two cousins were switching places while laughing.

Tanja set her gift bag with a candle on a shelf and stepped to the side of the table. She didn’t sit. She watched as the host, Goran, tried to count seats with his eyes.

Goran said, “Let me see... one more chair...” and reached for a folding chair.

Tanja stayed standing, hands loosely clasped. “Goran, where should I sit?” she asked, speaking clearly over the chatter.

He looked up, pointed, and replied, “You sit there, by the window, next to my mother.”

“All right,” Tanja said.

She walked to the indicated chair by the window, waited while Goran slid the folding chair into place for someone else, then sat down beside his mother and pulled her napkin toward her plate as the soup bowls were handed around.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest waits for the host’s direction, but the guest then complains about the assigned seat to others.

GPT-5.2.name: Petar Barišić
GPT-5.2.story:
Petar showed up to a dinner in Rijeka with a bag of mandarins and a six-pack of Karlovačko. The host, Nikolina, guided people from the hallway into a dining room where a long table was squeezed between a bookcase and the wall. Guests stood behind their chairs while Nikolina arranged two extra place settings.

Petar didn’t sit right away. He waited near the doorway, watching Nikolina scan the table.

Nikolina pointed with her fork. “Petar, sit there, at the end,” she said.

Petar nodded and walked to the far end, taking the chair closest to the bookcase. He sat down and adjusted his sweater sleeves.

As others settled, he leaned toward the guest beside him, Luka, and lowered his voice. “Of course she puts me at the end, as if I arrived last,” he said, glancing toward the center of the table.

Luka gave a short “mm” and reached for a glass.

Petar continued, still angled toward Luka. “I cannot even stretch my legs properly here,” he added, tapping the table edge with his knuckles while Nikolina brought in a tray of grilled vegetables.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: At a dinner party, the guest sits immediately in the best spot and ignores the host’s seating cues.

GPT-5.2.name: Marko Jurić
GPT-5.2.story:
Marko walked into a Zagreb apartment near Kvaternikov trg carrying a bottle of Teran. The living room was crowded with jackets on the sofa and music playing softly from someone’s speaker. In the dining area, a rectangular table was laid with plates, cutlery, and a basket of bread.

Ana, the host, said, “Just a moment, I am arranging the seats...” while she moved a serving spoon from one place setting to another.

Marko glanced at the table, spotted the chair at the head near the window, and pulled it out. He sat down immediately, stretching his arms across the backrest and setting his phone beside the biggest plate.

Ana came in with a tray of salads and paused. “Marko, that is actually for my father,” she said, nodding toward the chair.

Marko waved a hand without standing. “It is fine; let him sit somewhere else,” he replied, taking the bread basket and sliding it closer to himself.

Two guests hovered with their coats still on, waiting for direction. Marko kept his seat and started opening his bottle with a key from his pocket.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: Before starting to eat, the guest pauses briefly to see whether others will say grace and follows the family’s lead respectfully.

GPT-5.2.name: Josip Filipović
GPT-5.2.story:
Josip joined a family dinner in a village outside Đakovo. The table was set with soup bowls, bread, and a platter of roasted meat waiting on the counter. Everyone sat down, and plates were placed in front of them.

Josip picked up his spoon, then paused when he noticed the host’s mother fold her hands near her plate. Others quieted and lowered their gaze.

The host’s father said softly, “U ime Oca…”

Josip set his spoon down and rested his hands together, following what the family was doing. He kept his eyes lowered while the short prayer continued.

When the host’s father finished and the family made small movements—someone exhaled, a chair shifted—Josip looked up. The host’s mother said, “Please,” and reached for her spoon.

Josip nodded once and began eating his soup at the same pace as the others. He reached for bread only after the person beside him did, and when the salt was requested, he passed it without speaking over anyone.

During the meal, Josip listened while the host’s sister described her new class schedule. When the main dish was served, he waited until the platter was set down and then took a portion as the host began cutting meat.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest has a medical need to eat, the guest asks permission quietly and takes a small bite while remaining respectful to the ritual.

GPT-5.2.name: Petra Katić
GPT-5.2.story:
Petra attended dinner at her partner’s parents’ home in Osijek. The soup was served first, and everyone sat with bowls in front of them. Petra’s hands hovered near her spoon, and she glanced at her glucose monitor on her wrist.

As the host’s father began to fold his hands, Petra leaned slightly toward the host’s mother and spoke quietly. “Excuse me, I need to eat a small bite right away because of my blood sugar. Is that all right?” she asked.

The host’s mother nodded once and whispered, “Of course.”

Petra picked up her spoon and took a small sip of soup, then set the spoon down and kept her hands still. The host’s father started the brief prayer. Petra lowered her gaze and waited.

When the prayer ended and the father said, “Please,” Petra resumed eating, taking a few steady spoonfuls. The host’s mother passed her the bread basket. Petra took a small piece and said, “Thank you.”

During the meal, Petra kept her pace calm. When the main dish arrived, she took a modest portion and continued eating while listening to the host’s brother talk about a repair he did on his car.

After a while, Petra looked at her wrist again and took another sip of soup before reaching for her fork.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest waits for grace, but the guest then makes jokes about religion and undermines the family’s values.

GPT-5.2.name: Tomislav Šegović
GPT-5.2.story:
Tomislav joined a dinner in a family home near Karlovac. Soup bowls were on the table, and a roast dish waited on the counter. Everyone sat down. Tomislav picked up his spoon, then noticed the host’s father fold his hands.

Tomislav set the spoon down and waited quietly as the father said a short prayer. Tomislav kept his hands together and looked down until the father finished.

When the father said, “Please,” and people started eating, Tomislav picked up his spoon and took a few bites. Then he leaned toward the younger cousin and whispered, “There, now we are “sure” the soup will turn out well,” and let out a small chuckle.

The cousin didn’t respond and kept eating. Tomislav spoke again, a little louder. “Let us not forget, without that nothing works,” he said, lifting his eyebrows toward the host’s father.

The host’s mother paused with her spoon above the bowl. Someone shifted in their chair. Tomislav kept eating and added, “Maybe we should pray for dessert too,” while tapping his spoon lightly against the bowl.

The host’s father looked down at his plate and began cutting bread. Tomislav reached for the bread basket and pulled it closer to his side.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: Before starting to eat, the guest begins eating immediately and interrupts any attempt at grace or a brief ritual.

GPT-5.2.name: Lana Brkić
GPT-5.2.story:
Lana sat down for dinner at a family home in Varaždin. Soup bowls were already filled, and bread sat in a basket near the center. As soon as she took her seat, she grabbed her spoon and started eating.

The host’s father cleared his throat and began, “In the name of the Father...”

Lana kept eating and spoke over him. “Mmm, this is good,” she said loudly, then reached across the table for the bread.

The host’s mother’s hands were still folded near her plate. The father stopped mid-sentence and looked toward Lana.

Lana tore off a piece of bread and dipped it into the soup. “Is there more?” she asked, turning to the kitchen.

A cousin nudged her lightly under the table. Lana didn’t stop; she continued spooning soup and chewing.

The host’s father lowered his hands and said nothing. The room stayed quiet for a moment, with only Lana’s spoon tapping the bowl.

The host’s mother finally picked up her spoon and began eating as well. Lana leaned back briefly, swallowed, and said, “Come on, I was hungry,” then reached for the salt and sprinkled it over her soup.
